content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 25, 2008

Mario A. Vazquez
Paralegal
NuStar Energy LP
P.O. Box 781609
San Antonio, TX 78278

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-013

Dear Mr. Vazquez:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of NuStarpac
regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Does a contribution made to a U.S. Congressman in California trigger any
reporting under the Act?

CONCLUSION

No.

FACTS

NuStar Energy LP is a company that is headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. In
August 2007, they registered NuStarpac, a political action committee, in California. In
2007, the committee's only activity in California was a contribution to a U.S.
Congressman, which you indicate was appropriately reported to the Federal Election
Commission.

________________________
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-08-013
Page No. 2

ANALYSIS

The Act does not regulate contributions to federal candidates. (Sutton Advice
Letter, No. A-97-168; Reed Advice Letter, No. I-88-213.) The definition of "candidate"
in the Act expressly exempts "any person within the meaning of Section 301(b) of the
Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971." (Section 82007.) Thus, candidates for federal
office, such as Congress, are not reached by provisions applying to candidates otherwise
defined in Section 82007.

Because NuStarpac did not make contributions or expenditures in California
during 2007, they did not qualify as a California committee and no reporting was
triggered for that year. However, please note that once NuStarpac qualifies as a recipient
committee under the Act (Section 82013(a)), semi-annual statements must be filed for
each half of the year, whether or not they receive contributions or make expenditures
during the six-month period. In addition to semi-annual statements, additional reports
may be required during any year in which contributions or expenditures are made to
support or oppose California state or local candidates or ballot measures.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me
at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Tara L. Stock
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

TS:jgl
